label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Today is the second day of the third month of 1966. That would be March 2, 1966, right? Because the third month is March. So March has 31 days, I think. Now, the question is asking what the date was a month ago. So if today is March 2, 1966, then a month ago would be February 2, 1966. Wait, but why? Because if you subtract one month from March 2, you just go back to February 2. But I need to make sure that February 1966 actually had a 2nd day. Well, February always has 28 days in a non-leap year. 1966 is not a leap year because leap years are divisible by 4. 1966 divided by 4 is 491.5, so not a whole number. So February 1966 had 28 days. Therefore, February 2 is a valid date. Looking at the options, option C is 02/02/1966, so that seems correct. Let me check the options again: (A) 02/01/1966 – that's February 1st, not the 2nd. So not this one. (B) 12/17/1965 – December 17th, which is way earlier. Not a month ago. (C) 02/02/1966 – yes, that's February 2nd. Correct. (D)
